perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confid ential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors, and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Private Information in reasonable confidence for the benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall restrict access to Private Information to employees, contractors, and third parties as is necessary and shall require those persons to sign standard confidentiality agreements.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Information,' weakening 'strictest confidence' to 'reasonable confidence,' removing 'sole and exclusive,' changing 'reasonably required' to 'is necessary,' and replacing 'nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement' with 'standard confidentiality agreements' drastically reduces the protection afforded to the Disclosing Party's information. This contradicts the intent of a Non-Disclosure Agreement, creating uncertainty about the level of protection required. Also, not specifying a confidence interval and creating a contradiction within the scope in the requirements for security, due to the vagueness of the sentecne.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Time Periods. The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreemen t, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the protection of confidential information by changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Information,' weakening 'strictest confidence' to 'reasonable confidence,' removing 'sole and exclusive,' changing 'reasonably required' to 'is necessary,' and replacing 'nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement' with 'standard confidentiality agreements.' The contradicted text states that the Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret. The original text ensures strict confidentiality for an indefinite duration if the information remains a trade secret, while the changed text introduces a weaker standard ('reasonable confidence') and refers to 'Private Information' and 'standard confidentiality agreements,' creating a strong contradiction regarding the level and type of protection required, and thus creates a direct conflict and contradiction of the obligation and definition of term between these clauses.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Information immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party may retain copies of records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Information even if Disclosing Party requests their return in writing.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall return' to 'may retain copies' directly contradicts the standard obligation in an NDA to return confidential information upon request. This undermines the Disclosing Party's control over their information and creates a significant point of contention. The direct contradiction makes enforcement unpredictable. This also goes against the definition, because if its Proprietary Data or Private Information now, Recieving Party needs to return the information to Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Time Periods. The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreemen t, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, which allows the Receiving Party to retain copies of Confidential Information even after the Disclosing Party requests their return, directly contradicts the implied obligation to keep the Confidential Information secure, which is the entire point of the agreement as stated in the contradiction text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the changed text undermines the confidentiality and enforceability of the NDA, as it conflicts with the existing terms about the duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence even after termination.

perturbation.original_text: The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreemen t, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidentiality provisions of this agreement will terminate 3 years after the agreement's signing, regardless of trade secret status or written notice.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the 'Time Periods' section from indefinite protection (until no longer a trade secret or released in writing) to a fixed 3-year term creates a direct contradiction. This fundamentally alters the duration of confidentiality, clashing with the expectations set earlier in the agreement about the ongoing protection of 'Confidential Information', 'Private Information', or 'Proprietary Data' that the contract now defines.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Proprietary Data" shall include all information or material that has or could have commercial value or other utility in the business in which Disclosing Party is engaged. If Proprietary Data is in written form, the Disclosing Party may label or stamp the materials with the word "Confidential" or some similar warning.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the nondisclosure provisions survive termination of the agreement indefinitely until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice. The changed text introduces a fixed 3-year term for confidentiality, regardless of trade secret status or written notice. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which defines 'Proprietary Data' as information with potential commercial value, implying ongoing protection. The change creates a conflict because the protection of 'Proprietary Data' is no longer indefinite, as the contradicted text implies, but limited to 3 years, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
